document.title: VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS V2.x Datasheet

sourceSummary.oneLine: Variscite datasheet for the VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS V2.x NXP i.MX 8M Plus-based system-on-module.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Technical supporting document describing product hardware, interfaces, assembly options, electrical specifications, environmental specifications, legal notice, warranty terms, and contact information.
keyPoints: The VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS is a low-power, high-performance system-on-module based on the NXP i.MX 8M Plus family.

Feature summary lists NXP i.MX8M-PLUS SoC, up to 8GB LPDDR4 RAM, up to 128GB eMMC, Wi-Fi 802.11a/b/g/n/ac/ax, Bluetooth 5.4/BLE, two Ethernet interfaces, display, camera, audio, USB, PCIe, CAN, UART, I2C, SPI, GPIO, and industrial temperature range.
Hardware configuration options include EC, AC, WBD, WBE, BTPCM, COEX, WBRST, ANT2, SDEX, TP, DSI, CSI2, SAI1, and QSPI.
Electrical specifications include VCC_SOM operating range of 3.25 V to 3.45 V and typical 3.3 V.
Environmental specifications list commercial, extended, industrial, storage, and humidity ranges.

evidence.claim: The document is a VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS V2.x datasheet issued by Variscite Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS V2.x Datasheet; VARISCITE LTD.

evidence.claim: The datasheet revision is Rev. 1.04 dated 03/2025.
evidence.evidenceText: VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS_V2.x Datasheet Rev. 1.04, 03/2025

evidence.claim: Revision 1.04 is dated Mar 15, 2025.
evidence.evidenceText: 1.04 Mar 15, 2025 Pin 9 EC Assy field added; QSPI/SDEX notes added

evidence.claim: The VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS is based on the NXP i.MX 8M Plus family.
evidence.evidenceText: The product is based on the i.MX 8M Plus family

evidence.claim: The module uses an NXP i.MX8M-PLUS series SoC with 4 Cortex-A53 cores up to 1.8 GHz, one Cortex-M7 at 800 MHz, and one Hi-Fi DSP at 800 MHz.
evidence.evidenceText: NXP i.MX8M-PLUS series SOC; 4x Cortex A53 up to @ 1.8 GHz; 1x Cortex M7 @ 800 MHz; 1x Hi-Fi DSP @ 800 MHz

evidence.claim: The module supports up to 8GB LPDDR4 RAM and up to 128GB eMMC boot and storage.
evidence.evidenceText: Up to 8GB LPDDR4 RAM @ 2000Mhz; 8-bit up to 128GB eMMC boot and storage

evidence.claim: The module supports HDMI 2.0a, two LVDS interfaces, and one MIPI DSI interface.
evidence.evidenceText: Display Support; 2x LVDS interface 4-lane each; HDMI 2.0a; 1x MIPI DSI with up to 4 data lanes

evidence.claim: The module supports two 10/100/1000 Mbit/s Ethernet interfaces.
evidence.evidenceText: Networking; 2x 10/100/1000 Mbit/s Ethernet Interface

evidence.claim: Wireless options support Wi-Fi 802.11a/b/g/n/ac/ax and Bluetooth 5.4/BLE.
evidence.evidenceText: Certified Wi-Fi 802.11a/b/g/n/ac/ax; Bluetooth: 5.4/BLE

evidence.claim: The WBE option includes Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and 802.15.4; the WBD option includes Wi-Fi and Bluetooth.
evidence.evidenceText: WBD 2.4GHz & 5GHz Wi-Fi® + Bluetooth® Module assembled on SOM; WBE 2.4GHz & 5GHz Wi-Fi® + Bluetooth® + 802.15.4 Module assembled on SOM

evidence.claim: The module exposes a 200-pin SO-DIMM connector.
evidence.evidenceText: The VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS exposes a 200-pin SO-DIMM connector.

evidence.claim: The module uses a single 3.3 V power supply.
evidence.evidenceText: Single power supply: 3.3V

evidence.claim: The module dimensions are 67.6 mm x 33 mm x 3.4 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Dimensions (W x L x H): 67.6 mm x 33 mm x 3.4 mm

evidence.claim: The industrial operating temperature range is -40°C to 85°C.
evidence.evidenceText: Industrial Operating Temperature Range -40°C 85°C

evidence.claim: Commercial operating temperature range is 0°C to 70°C and extended operating temperature range is -25°C to 85°C.
evidence.evidenceText: Commercial Operating Temperature Range 0°C 70°C; Extended Operating Temperature Range -25°C 85°C

evidence.claim: The operating range for VCC_SOM is 3.25 V minimum, 3.3 V typical, and 3.45 V maximum.
The DSI assembly option exposes MIPI DSI pins instead of LVDS0 pins.
evidence.evidenceText: In “DSI” assembly option LVDS0 interface pins are not available.

evidence.claim: The CSI2 assembly option exposes the second MIPI CSI-2 interface instead of HDMI pins.
evidence.evidenceText: In “CSI2” assembly option HDMI interface pins are not available.

evidence.claim: QSPI and SD1 cannot be assembled simultaneously due to shared SOM pins.
evidence.evidenceText: Due to shared SOM pins QSPI and SD1 cannot be assembled simultaneously.

evidence.claim: Resistive touch controller cannot be assembled if the WBE option is selected.
evidence.evidenceText: Resistive touch Controller cannot be assembled if WBE option is selected.

evidence.claim: Some COEX, WBRST, ANT2, and BTPCM assembly options were not fully tested.
evidence.evidenceText: This assembly option was not fully tested yet; for further support, please contact sales@variscite.com

evidence.claim: The module can boot from internal eMMC Flash memory or external SD Card.
evidence.evidenceText: The VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS can be boot from the following sources: Internal source - eMMC Flash memory; External source - SD Card

evidence.claim: Variscite states products are not intended for life support systems, appliances, nuclear systems, or systems where malfunction can reasonably be expected to result in personal injury, death, or severe property or environmental damage.
evidence.evidenceText: Variscite products are not intended for use in life support systems, appliances, nuclear systems or systems where malfunction can reasonably be expected to result in personal injury, death or severe property or environmental damage.

evidence.claim: Variscite guarantees hardware products against defects in workmanship and material for one year from date of shipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Variscite guarantees hardware products against defects in workmanship and material for a period of one (1) year from the date of shipment.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but the provided source text is a Variscite datasheet and does not mention MFDS or a Korean regulatory authority.
ProductSignal is Unclassified; the source text identifies VAR-SOM-MX8M-PLUS as the product subject.
The document title page gives date as 03/2025 while revision history gives Rev. 1.04 date as Mar 15, 2025; both were preserved without inferring a single publication date beyond the text.
The OCR text contains minor errors and duplicated table numbering, such as DATASHHET in filename, 802.15.14 in a note, and repeated Table 20/Table 21 labels.
